I thought I'd check it out, since it's a free tech demo. Well, a tech demo that seems seriously out of date, that is. It doesn't look pretty, and it's extremely jittery.
And hand-tracking, where?? As soon as I put away my controllers, the first action I try to do, my hands freeze in place..

I don't know what some of these reviews are on about. The demo on the Quest 3 has some decently high rez graphics with real time lighting in different environments. It actually looks really good. Id definitely be interested in a full game.

reviews.review_title: Is the name supposed to be ironic
reviews.review_description:
If this is meant to showcase what the Quest 3 is capable of, it's a major letdown. The visuals are far below par—especially compared to something like Ghost Town. Character models are unimpressive, and the lip-syncing is particularly bad, which is unacceptable for a demo that's supposed to highlight the hardware.

Movement is frustrating—limited to teleportation, and even that only works when the app decides to allow it. Object interaction feels clunky: grabbing items is awkward, and throwing is downright terrible. Objects often stick to your hand, then drop limply in front of you rather than reacting naturally. Physics are lackluster too—objects float on top of each other instead of displaying proper collision.

To top it off, the whole experience looks blurry and low-res at a distance. The only real positive is that it’s free—but even then, it hardly feels worth the time.

reviews.review_title: Feels like a good start to something
reviews.review_description: Pretty good for a show case. The teleport to specific spot controls are horrible. Seated play if you do the free mode you will need a desk chair as you will need to be able to swivel/turn. The teleport to the stations controls for the ship will just randomly pick teleportation spot at times. Switching from helm to sail angle may throw you on deck instead of the closest teleport location. This is not an issue in the short story mode for the showcase as it force turns you after move.
It is an interesting and fun demo. Graphics are pretty good and enjoyable story.

I understand that this is a tech demo, but I'm not really impressed with any of the tech. You're told to go from one place where you do some superficially interactive task, then told to go to another one, repeat for a bit.
Your hands get stuck on scenery and props a lot, you have to wait for everyone to tell you exactly what to do before you're allowed to do the thing that was obvious to do from the start, and your "interactions" amounts to little more than allowing the cutscene to continue. The most difficult part of the game is figuring out where the location you're supposed to teleport to is. 
The rope mechanics (the most complicated interaction) range from humorously floppy to an absurd lack of animation effort (the harpoon gun rope is a ridiculous line, even when you miss and aim above the horizon)
It has the bones of something that could be impressive, but it's going to take a complete overhaul for the execution to live up to the ambition of the concept.
In the meantime it's free.

if this becomes a whole game i'm definitely going to play it i don't know what these other early accessors are talking about especially since it's early access you can't crap on something that isn't out yet especially if it's early access

Adding guns

This isn’t a good showcase for graphics or hand tracking interactions.

The art direction is amateurish, negating any positives from improved graphical rendering technologies. The performance is also sluggish at times on the Quest 3.

It doesn’t handle roomscale well, seems like it wasn’t even tested with natural turning or movement.   

The hand gesture interactions are extremely fiddly. I quit the demo and deleted it after a few minutes because the teleport mechanic was so hit or miss.

There are far better hand tracking and graphical showcase demos available.

It was a fun and cool vr 
experience

This is a great example of how not to do it.

The graphics are stuck in the middle:
Not photorealistic (due to anime characters and obviously limited poly count). At the same time it does not commit to a nice abstract art style. The fancy shaders used feel out of place.

The gameplay feels outdated and clunky. The story has nothing to offer in terms of surprising or emotional or meaningful writing.

Not worth the time, even for free.

With this being a showcase of the graphical capabilities of the headset I was expecting something of high quality with amazing graphics for VR on meta quest 3…whilst there were elements of high quality graphics I’d say most was just ok and some not so good. 

The puddles of water, some of the objects, and the sailers hand effect were really good. 
Generally the ship and characters were good. But the shadows were pixelated around the edges and some of the objects weren’t very good. 
The main issue were the amount of glitches - the rope acted like it was extremely light and flailing everywhere and clipping through things. I laughed when one of the characters walked in with no hat and then it flew into place. The chat bubbles didn’t know where they were supposed to be. The height was annoying because I tried to play it sat down but it would calibrate properly. 

Considering this was a showcase of what meta has to offer - it’s not very impressive. Red Matter one and two look so much better.

reviews.review_title: The cutting edge couldn't cut jello!
reviews.review_description: I don't understand why this was labeled as cutting edge. From the description I was really expecting something out of this world in terms of graphics, quality, movement, etc. I saw better on the Gear VR from 2017. Don't expect too much.
awesome visual and hand interaction showcase in a small seafaring narrative full of cool moments.
